title: Fermented Creamy Syrian Pork Belly Paella
description: A post-workout Syrian paella dish featuring fermented flavors, creamy texture, and pork belly slices. Perfect for a diabetic-friendly meal.
instructions: Cut pork belly into 1-inch slices and season with salt, black pepper, and cumin.
Heat a large paella pan over medium-high heat and sear pork belly slices until browned, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
In the same pan, cook garlic and onion until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add rice to the pan and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add fermented turnip juice, paprika, and salt. Stir to combine.
Add browned pork belly slices back into the pan, followed by heavy cream.
Transfer the pan to the preheated oven and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Remove the pan from the oven and garnish with chopped parsley.
Cover the pan with papillote and let rest for 5-10 minutes before serving.
